title: Tweet
description: Tweet schema from X API v2

attachments.description: Specifies the type of attachments (if any) present in this Tweet.

media_keys.description: A list of Media Keys for each one of the media attachments (if media are attached).

media_source_tweet_id.description: A list of Posts the media on this Tweet was originally posted in. For example, if the media on a tweet is re-used in another Tweet, this refers to the original, source Tweet..

poll_ids.description: A list of poll IDs (if polls are attached).

author_id.description: Unique identifier of this User. This is returned as a string in order to avoid complications with languages and tools that cannot handle large integers.

community_id.description: The unique identifier of this Community.

conversation_id.description: Unique identifier of this Tweet. This is returned as a string in order to avoid complications with languages and tools that cannot handle large integers.

display_text_range.description: Represent a boundary range (start and end zero-based indices) for the portion of text that is displayed for a post. `start` must be smaller than `end`. The start index is inclusive, the end index is exclusive.

editable_until.description: Time when Tweet is no longer editable.

edits_remaining.description: Number of times this Tweet can be edited.

is_edit_eligible.description: Indicates if this Tweet is eligible to be edited.

edit_history_tweet_ids.description: A list of Tweet Ids in this Tweet chain.

items.description: Annotation for entities based on the Tweet text.

allOf.description: Represents the data for the annotation.

geo.description: The location tagged on the Tweet, if the user provided one.

id.description: Unique identifier of this Tweet. This is returned as a string in order to avoid complications with languages and tools that cannot handle large integers.

in_reply_to_user_id.description: Unique identifier of this User. This is returned as a string in order to avoid complications with languages and tools that cannot handle large integers.

lang.description: Language of the Tweet, if detected by X. Returned as a BCP47 language tag.

non_public_metrics.description: Nonpublic engagement metrics for the Tweet at the time of the request.

impression_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been viewed.

note_tweet.description: The full-content of the Tweet, including text beyond 280 characters.

organic_metrics.description: Organic nonpublic engagement metrics for the Tweet at the time of the request.

impression_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been viewed.

like_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been liked.

reply_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been replied to.

retweet_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been Retweeted.

possibly_sensitive.description: Indicates if this Tweet contains URLs marked as sensitive, for example content suitable for mature audiences.

promoted_metrics.description: Promoted nonpublic engagement metrics for the Tweet at the time of the request.

impression_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been viewed.

like_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been liked.

reply_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been replied to.

retweet_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been Retweeted.

public_metrics.description: Engagement metrics for the Tweet at the time of the request.

bookmark_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been bookmarked.

impression_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been viewed.

like_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been liked.

quote_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been quoted.

reply_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been replied to.

retweet_count.description: Number of times this Tweet has been Retweeted.

referenced_tweets.description: A list of Posts this Tweet refers to. For example, if the parent Tweet is a Retweet, a Quoted Tweet or a Reply, it will include the related Tweet referenced to by its parent.

reply_settings.description: Shows who can reply a Tweet. Fields returned are everyone, mentioned_users, subscribers, verified and following.

followers.description: Indicates if this Tweet is viewable by followers without the Tweet ID

suggested_source_links_with_counts.description: Suggested source links and the number of requests that included each link.

count.description: Number of note requests that included the source link.

text.example: Learn how to use the user Tweet timeline and user mention timeline endpoints in the X API v2 to explore Tweet\u2026 https:\/\/t.co\/56a0vZUx7i

username.description: The X handle (screen name) of this user.

copyright.description: Indicates if the content is being withheld for on the basis of copyright infringement.

country_codes.description: Provides a list of countries where this content is not available.

scope.description: Indicates whether the content being withheld is the `tweet` or a `user`.
